question: Russia-Ukraine Ceasefire before GTA VI?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if  if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Only ceasefires which constitute a general pause in the conflict will qualify. Ceasefires which only apply to energy infrastructure, the Black Sea, or other similar agreements will not qualify.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution for a ceasefire will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Drake's Iceman album dropping before GTA VI's locked November 19, 2026 U.S. launch, propelled by Drake's March 30 speech confirming an imminent release amid his year-long teaser campaign and re-recording efforts to perfect the project. Rockstar Games has maintained radio silence on Trailer 3 since last summer's drop, dismissing April delay rumors and reiterating focus on polish after prior postponements from 2025 to May then November. Bets also spotlight GPT-6 previews eyed for mid-2026 and Rihanna's studio return in February, underscoring entertainment's rapid shifts; summer marketing could accelerate GTA VI momentum, but album rollouts remain key swing factors.

question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
description:

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Harvey Weinstein's ultimate prison time reflects deep uncertainty from his ongoing New York retrials and California appeals, with no outcome exceeding 27% implied probability amid his nearly six years incarcerated at Rikers Island. The leading "No Prison Time" at 27% gains traction from successful prior conviction overturns, his fragile health at age 74, and defense hints at plea deals for concurrent sentencing, while 20-30 years (23.5%) and 10-20 years (20.8%) contend due to his upheld 2025 New York sexual assault conviction (up to 25 years potential) atop the standing 16-year California rape term. Key swing factors include the April 14 Manhattan rape retrial—his third on that charge—and pending appeals, where judicial leniency or upsets could deadlock further as #MeToo legal precedents evolve.

eventMetadata.context_description: As the 2025-26 NHL regular season nears its April close, trader consensus on Polymarket crowns the Colorado Avalanche at 20.1% implied probability to win the 2026 Stanley Cup, buoyed by their Central Division lead—bolstered three days ago by a key regulation win over the Dallas Stars—and Nathan MacKinnon's scoring surge amid a healthy roster post-trade deadline. Tampa Bay Lightning (14.5%) and Carolina Hurricanes (12.5%) trail closely, locked into top Atlantic and Metropolitan spots with 99-100% playoff odds, elite goaltending from Andrei Vasilevskiy and Frederik Andersen, and deadline depth additions fueling late momentum. This bunched top tier reflects Eastern parity, multiple clinched berths for Buffalo Sabres and Montreal Canadiens, and playoffs' inherent volatility where upsets abound despite Western edge.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 39.5% implied probability to repeat as NBA champions after clinching the top Western Conference seed with a league-best 62-16 record, anchored by the NBA's No. 1 defense (106.0 DefRtg) and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play driving a +11.6 NetRtg. San Antonio Spurs follow at 14.9% following their franchise-record 59-19 mark and runner-up finish in the 2025 NBA Cup, fueled by Victor Wembanyama's dominance alongside acquired De'Aaron Fox for elite scoring (118.6 ORtg, No. 5). Boston Celtics sit third at 11.7% with a 53-25 Eastern Conference record and playoff pedigree, while Denver Nuggets (10.5%) rely on Nikola Jokić's triple-double averages amid a tight No. 3 West race. Detroit Pistons' surprising East lead (57-22) yields just 5.5% due to relative inexperience versus contenders' depth and postseason track records, with no major injuries altering late-season clinchings.
